title: Joint MEG-EEG Source Imaging
sub_domain: Multi-modal bioelectromagnetic neural source localization (multi-physics joint inverse)
source_data_quality_note: v3 standalone multi-physics genesis Principle. Joint forward operator couples Maxwell magnetostatic Biot-Savart (MEG) with Poisson electrostatic in a layered head-volume-conductor model (EEG). Recovers spatiotemporal neural current dipole density J(r, t) from simultaneous MEG and EEG measurements. The joint inverse is better-conditioned than either modality alone — MEG is sensitive to tangential cortical sources, EEG to radial sources; combined acquisition triangulates both classes. Distinct from L1-067 MEG (single-modality, magnetic only) and L1-068 EEG (single-modality, electric only) by primitive set, sensing_mechanism, and the joint inversion formulation. Authored under the standalone-as-default v3 design (see pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/PWM_V3_STANDALONE_VS_COMPOSITE.md).
related_principles: L1-067 (MEG — magnetic-only neural source localization, d_principle ~ 0.50)
L1-068 (EEG — electric-only neural source localization, d_principle ~ 0.50)
L1-030 (MRI — head anatomical model component, informational)

E.description: Joint MEG-EEG Source Imaging: joint multi-physics forward couples (i) Maxwell magnetostatic Biot-Savart law for the magnetic field from intracranial neural currents B(r_sensor, t) = integral G_M(r_sensor, r'; sigma_head) * J(r', t) dr'; (ii) Poisson electrostatic equation for the scalp potential V(r_electrode, t) = integral G_E(r_electrode, r'; sigma_head) * J(r', t) dr' in a layered head-volume-conductor model (brain, CSF, skull, scalp); (iii) shared anatomical model parameterized by head conductivity profile sigma_head(r) derived from MR-derived segmentation. The forward DAG has 8 primitives with two coupling constraints (n_c = 2): (i) joint observation — the same neural source J(r, t) is simultaneously observed by MEG sensors (magnetic field B) and EEG electrodes (scalp potential V); (ii) shared head conductivity model — both Green's functions G_M and G_E depend on the same sigma_head(r), creating a structural coupling that improves identifiability when both modalities are jointly inverted. Recovery is posed as the joint linear inverse problem y(t) = [B(t); V(t)] = [G_M; G_E] * J(t) + n(t) where the stacked operator [G_M; G_E] has lower effective condition number than either G_M or G_E alone. Difficulty tier delta = 5 with raw condition number kappa ~ 1000 (the inverse problem is fundamentally underdetermined — finite sensors, infinitely many sources) and effective kappa_eff ~ 100 after Tikhonov / minimum-norm / sLORETA / beamformer regularization. Mismatch parameters: head_segmentation_error, conductivity_uncertainty, sensor_position_error, source_orientation_assumption, reference_electrode_drift, magnetic_artifact_contamination. Additive Gaussian thermal noise sets the data-fidelity floor. See forward_model field for the closed-form joint imaging equation.
E.forward_model: B(r_sensor, t) = (mu_0 / 4*pi) * integral [J(r', t) x (r_sensor - r') / |r_sensor - r'|^3] dr' (Biot-Savart, MEG forward); V(r_electrode, t) = integral G_E(r_electrode, r'; sigma_head) * div(J(r', t)) dr' (Poisson, EEG forward); jointly invert y(t) = [B(t); V(t)] = [G_M; G_E] * J(t) + n(t) -> J(r, t) given head model sigma_head(r)
E.world_state_x: Spatiotemporal neural current dipole density J(r, t) [A/m^2] on the cortical mesh (typically 5000-15000 dipoles distributed on the gray-matter surface) at millisecond temporal resolution. Recovers both source location and time course.
E.observation_y: Joint multi-channel measurement y(t) = [B(t); V(t)] with B = N_MEG_channels (typically 102-306 magnetometers/gradiometers) and V = N_EEG_channels (typically 32-256 electrodes); time series at 1000 Hz sampling.

G.dag: L.neural_source -> L.cortical_mesh_constraint -> L.head_volume_conductor -> L.maxwell_magnetostatic -> L.poisson_electrostatic -> L.megsensor_detection -> L.eegsensor_detection -> int.spatial -> int.temporal
coupling_constraints: joint_source_observation: same neural source J(r, t) observed simultaneously by MEG (B) and EEG (V); MEG is sensitive to tangential cortical sources, EEG to radial — combined modalities resolve both classes; coupling reduces effective null space
shared_head_conductivity: both Green's functions G_M and G_E depend on the same sigma_head(r) profile; structural coupling that improves joint identifiability when sigma_head is co-estimated

W.regime: Existence of recovered neural source distribution J(r, t) is guaranteed within the cortical-mesh constraint and the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness is fundamentally conditional — the joint inverse problem is underdetermined (~15000 source dipoles with constrained orientation vs ~400 sensors typical), so unique solutions require regularization (minimum-norm, sLORETA, beamformer, sparse priors, dynamical / temporal constraints). Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~ 100 after L2 regularization) — head_segmentation_error dominates source-localization bias; conductivity_uncertainty contributes scaling factor; sensor_position_error contributes a few-millimeter localization shift; source_orientation_assumption (free vs cortically-constrained vs surface-normal) contributes prior bias. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled MEG+EEG forward (with regularization) is established by Mosher et al. 1992, Hamalainen-Ilmoniemi 1994, Pascual-Marqui 2002 (sLORETA), Sharon et al. 2007, Henson et al. 2009, and Huang et al. 2014.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [minimum-norm estimator MNE; sLORETA; eLORETA; LCMV beamformer; mixed-norm priors] | nonlinear [dynamic causal modeling DCM; particle filtering] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [DeepSourceNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||J_hat - J||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_MEG + N_EEG) at each time t. Underdetermined-system bias adds + C2 * |head_segmentation_error| + C3 * |conductivity_uncertainty| + C4 * ||J - J_s||_1 (best s-sparse approximation residual under L1 prior). MNE / sLORETA achieve q=2 (O(1/k^2)) under L2 prior; mixed-norm solvers achieve q=1 linear convergence under L1 + L2 priors (sparse spatially, smooth temporally). Joint MEG+EEG inversion has 2-3x lower spatial localization error than single-modality MNE under typical head models. Deep-prior variants achieve faster initial convergence with bounded systematic bias.
C.complexity: O(N_dipoles * (N_MEG + N_EEG) * T_samples) per iteration via direct Green's-function evaluation; FEM-based BEM approaches O(N_dipoles * N_BEM_nodes^2) per forward; learned variants O(N_dipoles * F_theta_cost) per forward pass

joint_well_posedness_references: Mosher JC, Lewis PS, Leahy RM (1992) Multiple dipole modeling and localization from spatio-temporal MEG data. IEEE Trans Biomed Eng 39(6):541-557.
Hamalainen MS, Ilmoniemi RJ (1994) Interpreting magnetic fields of the brain: minimum norm estimates. Med Biol Eng Comput 32(1):35-42.
Pascual-Marqui RD (2002) Standardized low-resolution brain electromagnetic tomography (sLORETA): technical details. Methods Find Exp Clin Pharmacol 24 Suppl D:5-12.
Sharon D, Hamalainen MS, Tootell RB, Halgren E, Belliveau JW (2007) The advantage of combining MEG and EEG: comparison to fMRI in focally stimulated visual cortex. NeuroImage 36(4):1225-1235.
Henson RN, Mouchlianitis E, Friston KJ (2009) MEG and EEG data fusion: simultaneous localisation of face-evoked responses. NeuroImage 47(2):581-589.
Hauk O, Wakeman DG, Henson R (2011) Comparison of noise-normalized minimum norm estimates for MEG analysis using multiple resolution metrics. NeuroImage 54(3):1966-1974.
Huang MX, Mosher JC, Leahy RM (2014) A novel integrated MEG and EEG analysis method for dipolar sources. NeuroImage 98:134-149.
Gramfort A, Luessi M, Larson E et al. (2014) MNE software for processing MEG and EEG data. NeuroImage 86:446-460.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-067 MEG inverts only B(t) with sensing_mechanism = magnetic_only; n_c = 0; primitives focus on Biot-Savart only. L1-068 EEG inverts only V(t) with sensing_mechanism = electric_only; n_c = 0; primitives focus on Poisson electrostatic only. L1-507 jointly inverts the stacked observation [B; V] with sensing_mechanism = joint_magnetic_and_electric_with_volume_conductor; n_c = 2 (joint source observation + shared head model); primitives include both Maxwell magnetostatic and Poisson electrostatic plus a unified L.head_volume_conductor coupling. Solution_space is 5D_neural_source_spatiotemporal vs the single-modality 4D versions. Jaccard distance over (sensing_mechanism, primitives, problem_class) ~ 0.50 from each parent — solidly Distinct under v1 advisory thresholds (>= 0.30). Clinical motivation: combined MEG+EEG has 2-3x lower spatial localization error than either alone, with measurable benefit in epilepsy presurgical mapping and evoked-response studies.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Clinical: epilepsy presurgical mapping (interictal-spike source localization), evoked-response studies (somatosensory/auditory/visual mapping for tumor resection planning), brain-computer interfaces. Joint MEG-EEG offers measurable advantage over either modality alone — MEG sensitive to tangential cortical sources, EEG to radial; combined acquisition triangulates both classes with 2-3x lower mean spatial localization error in benchmark validation. Established at major academic medical centers with co-located MEG and EEG capabilities (Mass General, Karolinska, Aston, etc.). Standard reference implementations: MNE-Python, Brainstorm, FieldTrip, NUTMEG.
